content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Persist next attempt, last success, failure streak, publisher guidance, pause cause, and schedule reason outside process timers.

hints: For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Persisted Due State model · Theory Feed Ts Persi…, name the reader, publisher, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, tenant, URL, unknown XML, runtime validation, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, PostgreSQL, Express, handle, accessibility, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Feed Ts Persisted Due State precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Persisted Due State model · Theory Feed Ts Persi…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 5474 during a cross-tenant privacy and cache audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove durable due state, injected clocks, non-overlapping timers, backoff, pause, and handle cleanup; every external value must remain unknown until current runtime validation admits it; then change one PostgreSQL constraint, transaction, migration, upsert, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Persist next attempt, last success, failure streak, publisher guidance, pause cause, and schedule reason outside process timers.

For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Persisted Due State model · Theory Feed Ts Persi…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: setInterval is a durable job scheduler.
